title: Engineering handoff export and case packaging
description: Turn the current incident packet into a real support-to-engineering deliverable. Export a clean engineering case package that includes incident framing, runtime-backed versus inferred-only evidence, replay result, chosen mitigation and fallback, suspected owner/file/function, debugger trail where available, and post-approval outcome summary. The packet should be understandable outside the incident UI and feel ready for a Jira, GitHub issue, or Slack escalation workflow.
done_when: the incident can produce a concise engineering handoff export that bundles the core evidence and action context
the export clearly distinguishes runtime-backed evidence from inferred-only reasoning
the exported case packet is readable without requiring the engineer to interpret raw NEXUS UI sections

title: INC002 bounded debugger flow parity
description: Bring bounded debugger depth closer to parity for the DB pool exhaustion outage. Add an explicit ordered debugger flow for `INC002` with concrete state checkpoints, expected transitions, and a truthful bounded scope tied to the curated Postgres pack. Keep it specific to the known outage class rather than pretending to support general-purpose database debugging.
done_when: INC002 has a bounded debugger flow with concrete checkpoints and ordered reasoning
the debugger flow is clearly tied to the curated DB pool outage pack and explicitly bounded
the incident UI and docs make INC001 and INC002 feel like two real supported debugging flows rather than one real flow and one generic trace

title: Runtime-host productization v2
description: Go deeper than the current visibility layer and make runtime-host feel like a fuller product capability. Add stronger operator controls or views for runtime mode, pack selection context, execution eligibility, and coverage boundaries. The goal is to make the bounded reproduction layer feel intentionally productized, not just surfaced.
done_when: runtime-host posture feels like an intentional product layer rather than a hidden technical detail
operators can understand replay eligibility, pack context, and execution boundaries from the product surfaces
the runtime-host story is clearer on more than one screen, not only the incident detail view

title: Outcome-weighted memory ranking
description: Make the memory layer prefer what actually worked. Use approved or executed outcomes to rank similar incidents, mitigations, and learned guidance so that future cases are grounded more in proven actions and less in generic similarity alone.
done_when: memory ranking gives visible preference to prior cases with successful executed outcomes
the product can explain that a recalled memory was favored because its mitigation actually worked
FORGE, GUARDIAN, or memory sections visibly benefit from outcome-weighted ranking

title: Curated pack onboarding contract
description: Define how a new outage class becomes a curated runtime and debugger pack. This should include the minimum required metadata, replay contract, debugging checkpoints, ownership hints, and operator-facing coverage description. The goal is to make NEXUS extensible in a disciplined way instead of growing through ad hoc hardcoding.
done_when: the repo contains a clear onboarding contract for new curated outage packs
the contract explains what REPLICA, TRACE, and debugger support require for a new outage class
the product or docs can truthfully describe how new supported outage classes get added

title: Operator ROI and audit surface
description: Expose the product value more explicitly: manual relay reduction, replay coverage, approval outcomes, recurrence signals, and memory reuse. The operator and owner should be able to see why this system is useful operationally, not just what a single incident did.
done_when: the product shows at least one operator-facing surface summarizing support-triage value and audit outcomes
approval or execution outcomes, replay coverage, and memory reuse are visible in a high-signal way
the value story is legible without reading long explanatory docs

title: Visual system reinvention pass
description: Return to the deferred UI ambition and push beyond the successful refinement from item 35. This pass should aim for a more distinctive and memorable product look while preserving the operational clarity gained in items 35 and 36. Treat it as a deliberate design pass, not random cosmetic change.
done_when: the product feels materially more distinctive than the current visual refinement pass
core screens preserve or improve the current information clarity while achieving a stronger visual identity
the redesign feels intentional and product-grade rather than theme swapping
